typecheck.note: Success: no issues found in 94 source files

appliedUpdates.description: Add knowledge entry: Flet's CanvasKit renderer doesn't expose DOM elements, making Playwright browser automation impossible. All E2E tests use component-level mocking with unittest.mock as a workaround.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add E2E testing convention: For Flet desktop apps, E2E tests use component-level mocking with unittest.mock (not Playwright browser automation) because Flet's CanvasKit renderer doesn't expose DOM elements. Tests import Flet screen classes directly and verify component structure via MagicMock.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All 6 E2E test files (test_history_page.py, test_session_detail_page.py, test_settings_page.py, test_home_page.py, test_profile_page.py, test_session_page.py) follow this same pattern. The e2e-worker skill and AGENTS.md say Playwright but all tests use mock-based component testing.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that for Flet desktop apps, Playwright browser automation is not possible due to CanvasKit DOM limitation. Provide mock-based component testing procedure as the correct approach.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: e2e-history-page, e2e-session-detail-page, e2e-settings-page all deviated from the skill's Playwright procedure. All 6 E2E test files in the project use the same mock-based component approach.
